artifact.schema_coverage: none; amendment mappings are outside source model 0.2.0 and the current JSON Schema/validator (DEC-021, DEC-023, DEC-025)
artifact.review_note: Reviewed effective-state synthesis is NOT part of this artifact; see Module 3.4.

amendment_mappings.mapped_scope: How a pharmacologically relevant species is determined.
amendment_mappings.analyst_rationale: Part I 3.3 defines a relevant species (pharmacological activity via receptor/epitope) and states that a variety of techniques can identify one. Part II 2.1 explains how that determination is made: target sequence homology as a starting point, in vitro binding/occupancy comparisons, and functional-activity assessment. The Addendum records do not change the Parent definition; they explain and disambiguate the evidence used to satisfy it.

amendment_mappings.mapped_scope: Number of species for the general toxicology program.
amendment_mappings.analyst_rationale: Part I 3.3 states that safety evaluation programs should normally include two relevant species, with one sufficing in certain justified cases. Part II 2.2 constrains that general statement into specific conditions: both species for short-term studies when two relevant species exist, one species usually sufficient for longer-term studies, one species when the candidate is active in only one species, and an explicit restriction that studies in two non-rodent species are not appropriate. The two-non-rodent restriction and the duration-based split limit the applicability of the Parent's general two-species expectation, so the relation is narrows rather than a plain clarification.

amendment_mappings.mapped_scope: Approach when no relevant species can be identified.
amendment_mappings.analyst_rationale: Part I 3.3 states that when no relevant species exists, relevant transgenic animals expressing the human receptor or homologous proteins should be considered. Part II 2.1 begins with "As described in ICH S6 Guideline" and restates the same alternative pathway. The associated condition ich_s6_r1.cond.part2.2_1.004 preserves the orthologous-target clause as material evidence: it explains a specific mechanism by which no relevant species can be identified, but does not add a new alternative, independent obligation, or broader scope. Therefore, the Addendum disambiguates the Parent condition rather than supplementing it with new guidance. Amendment mappings currently use KnowledgeRecord endpoints; linked Conditions must also be followed during effective-state synthesis.

amendment_mappings.mapped_scope: Role and permissible use of tissue cross-reactivity evidence in species selection
amendment_mappings.analyst_rationale: Part I 3.3 treats tissue cross-reactivity evidence as part of species relevance for monoclonal antibodies (kr.part1.3_3.004), and allows an epitope-negative species to remain of some relevance where comparable unintentional tissue cross-reactivity to humans is demonstrated (kr.part1.3_3.005). Addendum body records kr.part2.2_1.007 and kr.part2.2_1.008 carry the primary species-selection guidance: animal-tissue tissue cross-reactivity is of limited value for species selection, and may be used only in specific cases where other approaches cannot identify a pharmacologically relevant species. That body source unit explicitly references Note 1 (see the contextual cross-reference below), and Note 1 records kr.part2.notes.005 and kr.part2.notes.014 materially define the restriction: full-panel animal-tissue tissue cross-reactivity is not recommended, and selected animal-tissue evaluation provides only conditional supplemental information (when there is unexpected binding in human tissues). Including the Note 1 records as Addendum endpoints does not mean Note 1 independently amends the Parent; the body records and the referenced Note 1 records together form the complete Addendum meaning for this mapping. Taken together, the Addendum neither contradicts nor replaces the Parent guidance: it narrows the scope and evidentiary role of animal-tissue tissue cross-reactivity in species selection, so the relation type is narrows.
amendment_mappings.contextual_cross_reference_note: kr.part2.2_1.008 derives from source unit su.part2.2_1.005, which carries the explicit reference xref.part2.2_1.001 with raw text '(see Note 1)'. The Note 1 target is not model-resolvable (target_id=null, resolution_status=needs_review), but the raw reference and the related Note 1 source units are preserved, so the contextual linkage is present and was reviewed before assigning the relation type.
